title: Makefile: use order-only prereq for podman-remote
body:
podman-remote has a dependency on $(SRCBINDIR), because on
Mac and Windows that's a special dir that may not exist.
But depending on a directory means depending on its mtime,
which changes every time a file in it is updated, which
means running 'make' twice in a row will rebuild podman-remote
for no good reason.

Solution: GNU Make has the concept of "order-only" prerequisites,
precisely for this situation. Use it. Since it's an obscure
feature, document it.

Fixes: #14756

(Also, drive-by edit to remove a stray misdocumented non-option)

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>
```release-note
minor Makefile cleanup to eliminate an unnecessary duplicate build of podman-remote
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Fingers crossed that this works on Mac. I don't think Mac has GNU make.

OS X is green, and I see the [mkdir -p in the log](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/4549324158795776?logs=build_amd64#L214), so this is going to pass (except for the deadlock bug, should it choose to manifest, but that can be restarted)

WOW! This one's a doozy, and it's a real bug in our process.

Something somewhere seems to be calling `make podman-remote-linux`. I think it's the `make release` here: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/d890c4d98edcd2ed6f5f26bb19ed2282cea1cb46/contrib/cirrus/runner.sh#L234-L238

But, and this is a huge but, `podman-remote-linux` is not the same as `podman-remote`!

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/d890c4d98edcd2ed6f5f26bb19ed2282cea1cb46/Makefile#L329-L335

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/d890c4d98edcd2ed6f5f26bb19ed2282cea1cb46/Makefile#L305-L311

The biggest difference I see is that `podman-remote-linux` runs with `CGO_ENABLED=0`. 

Prior to my fix here, this didn't matter, because a subsequent step rebuilt `podman-remote` (because of the mtime bug that this PR tries to fix).

At this point, fixing this is way above my head. My inclination is to get rid of all the horrible duplicate confusing nasty inconsistent `podman-remote-*` rules, and change:
```diff
- podman-remote-%-docs: podman-remote-$(call err_if_empty,NATIVE_GOOS)
+ podman-remote-%-docs: podman-remote
```
...but that's a bigger change than I feel comfortable with. @containers/podman-maintainers help please.

@containers/podman-maintainers this is ready for careful review.

My one-line fix uncovered serious problems with our Makefile, which I have attempted to address by excising unnecessary (and confusing and duplicaty) targets. I think the end result is cleaner and easier to understand, but there may be cases outside of CI where it breaks. I have no access to non-linux OSes, so I have no way of testing further.
